sourceFiles.name: vehicle-model

fix.title: Add requirement satisfaction and verification traceability for PerformanceReq and SafetyReq
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by introducing explicit `satisfy` relationships from relevant design elements to both requirements and `verify` relationships from verification cases to those requirements. The requirements are therefore no longer untraced or unresolved, satisfying the criterion that every requirement must have at least one satisfy or verify relationship unless explicitly marked pending/TBD.

changes.description: Updated the Vehicle definition to add explicit `satisfy` relationships from relevant design elements to the two requirements, establishing structural satisfaction traceability.
Added verification definitions with explicit `verify` relationships to both requirements so each requirement has verification coverage in addition to satisfaction traceability.

fix.title: Add stakeholder and derived non-functional requirements with refinement and verification coverage
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by expanding the model from two isolated requirements to a coherent requirement set that includes stakeholder-level non-functional requirements for performance, safety, reliability, maintainability, and interoperability, plus derived requirements linked via `derivedReq` and refinement relationships. Each requirement now has a subject, descriptive text, and at least one `verify` relationship through explicit verification cases, making the requirement baseline directly actionable for downstream design and verification planning.

changes.description: Replaced the minimal two-requirement section with a complete stakeholder and derived requirement baseline covering performance, safety, reliability, maintainability, and interoperability, and added derivation/refinement and verification relationships required for requirements coverage completeness.

fix.title: Add explicit vehicle system context and boundary model
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by introducing a top-level context model that explicitly identifies the vehicle as the system of interest, models external actors/systems/environment outside the vehicle boundary, and defines boundary-crossing interfaces as ports and connections. The update preserves the existing vehicle structure and use case while adding a production-ready context representation suitable for context analysis and interface identification.

changes.description: Extended the Vehicle boundary definition with explicit context-facing ports so the system of interest has identifiable interfaces crossing its boundary.
Updated the existing Driver definition to participate in the boundary model through an explicit control interface.
Added external systems/environment parts and context interface port definitions to represent actors, infrastructure, environment, and traffic external to the vehicle.
Added an explicit completeness requirement for boundary/context representation so the model contains a governed artifact addressing the review finding.
Added a top-level VehicleSystemContext part and an example usage instance to explicitly show the vehicle boundary and all modeled external interactions.

fix.title: Add explicit vehicle boundary ports, external context elements, and context connections
fix.description: This fix resolves the system-boundary completeness deficiency by making the top-level system boundary explicit on Vehicle, introducing modeled external counterpart systems/actors, and connecting the boundary-facing vehicle ports to those external elements in a context view. The existing internal intent is preserved by connecting Vehicle.engine.powerPort to Vehicle.powerOut and then exposing Vehicle.powerOut to an external EnergyConsumer in the vehicle context. Driver interaction is also represented through a control boundary port and connected external actor, so the model now shows boundary-facing interfaces and their external counterparts as required.

changes.description: Updated Vehicle to expose an additional top-level boundary port for driver interaction and connected the internal engine port to the top-level power boundary port so the system boundary and internal interface path are explicit.
Extended Driver with an explicit boundary-facing control port so it can act as an external counterpart in the context view.
Added explicit interface and external system definitions plus a context requirement to capture the boundary completeness obligation.
Added concrete external instances and a vehicle context view that models the system boundary explicitly and connects Vehicle boundary ports to external counterparts.

fix.title: Add explicit structural context instances for wheels, driver, and subsystem
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by making all in-scope structural definitions participate in an explicit composition hierarchy. The vehicle instance now explicitly configures all four wheel instances under the existing `wheels : Wheel[4]` part usage, a new `VehicleOperationalContext` part definition provides a structural context that instantiates both a `vehicle` and a `driver`, and a direct `Subsystem` instance is added so the reusable base type is also structurally instantiated. This preserves the original model intent while removing ambiguity about whether these definitions are intended to be used structurally.

changes.description: Added a structural context definition that explicitly instantiates a vehicle, a driver, and a direct Subsystem usage so these definitions are represented in composition rather than only as reusable types or actors.
Expanded the existing `myCar` vehicle instance to explicitly configure all four wheel instances under `wheels : Wheel[4]`, removing ambiguity about wheel instantiation coverage.
Added an explicit operational context instance that structurally instantiates the driver and a direct Subsystem usage alongside the vehicle instance.

fix.title: Add directional power flow semantics to PowerPort and declare explicit port directions
fix.description: This fix resolves the interface completeness deficiency by updating the shared `PowerPort` interface to include explicit directional features (`out suppliedVoltage`, `out suppliedCurrent`) and by declaring concrete port directions on both usages: `Vehicle::powerOut` and `Engine::powerPort` are now `out` ports. This makes flow direction, causality, and compatibility analyzable at the interface definition and usage levels without changing the broader model structure.

changes.description: Declared explicit outgoing direction on the vehicle power interface usage so the boundary port has analyzable causality.
changes.before: port powerOut : PowerPort;
changes.after: out port powerOut : PowerPort;

changes.description: Declared explicit outgoing direction on the engine power interface usage so the component port has analyzable causality.
changes.before: port powerPort : PowerPort;
changes.after: out port powerPort : PowerPort;

changes.description: Replaced undirected port attributes with directed interface features in `PowerPort`, defining explicit outbound power-flow semantics shared by all uses of the port type.

fix.title: Connect engine power port to vehicle power output in Vehicle usage context
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by adding an explicit `connect` relationship in the relevant usage context (`myCar : Vehicle`) so that the previously unconnected `Vehicle::powerOut` and `Engine::powerPort` are connected when the vehicle is instantiated. It makes the internal power transfer path analyzable and removes the architecture incompleteness caused by declared but unused ports.

changes.description: Added a `connect` statement in the `myCar : Vehicle` usage context to connect the engine's power port to the vehicle's power output port, resolving the unconnected interface completeness defect.

fix.title: Add explicit boundary interfaces, external systems, and context-level connections
fix.description: This fix resolves the interface and connection completeness deficiency by expanding the model from a single minimal PowerPort to explicit boundary interface definitions for the significant vehicle context crossings, then using those interfaces in concrete external parts and explicit context-level connections. The updated model introduces directional, typed ports for propulsion energy, driver control, and road contact interactions; adds external systems and environment elements; and connects the vehicle to compatible counterparts in a context structure so system boundary crossings are no longer implicit.

changes.description: Replaced the minimal PowerPort with a fully typed interface using explicit directional flow items, and added additional significant boundary interface definitions for driver control and road interaction.
Updated the Vehicle boundary to include all significant external interfaces instead of only a single power port.
Extended external elements so the system boundary has compatible counterpart interfaces for actual context-level connections.
Added an explicit requirement capturing the expectation for defined and connected boundary interfaces, addressing the completeness finding with a model artifact traceable for future review.
Added a context-level structure with explicit compatible connections across the system boundary for driver, charging source, and road environment.

fix.title: Type analysis-relevant attributes with unit-bearing value definitions and explicit bounds
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness deficiency by replacing bare `Real` analysis attributes with explicit value types and by adding bound/nominal requirement artifacts for all critical quantitative properties called out in the finding. Vehicle, Engine, Transmission, Wheel, Subsystem, Driver, and PowerPort attributes now use dedicated value definitions that encode units semantically, and the model includes requirement definitions with subjects and quantitative constraints for mass, power, displacement, gear ratio, efficiency, wheel size, speed, and electrical interface limits. Instance values are retained and aligned to the new typed attributes, making the model directly usable for sizing, performance, and consistency analysis.

changes.description: Replaced bare `Real` analysis attributes with explicit unit-bearing value definitions so analysis-relevant properties are typed and semantically unambiguous.

Added requirement definitions with subjects and quantitative constraints to specify bounds and nominal values for all critical analysis attributes cited in the finding, including interface detail for PowerPort.
Completed leaf-level instance specification for wheel attributes so all major analysis-relevant properties are populated consistently with the new typed model.

fix.title: Elaborate DriveCar with executable action flow and supporting scenario requirement
fix.description: This fix resolves the behaviour completeness deficiency by turning the DriveCar use case from an objective-only stub into an elaborated operational use case with an explicit action flow. A dedicated action definition models the nominal sequence for starting, moving, monitoring, steering, and stopping the vehicle, and the use case now performs that behaviour. A supporting requirement is added so the behavioural elaboration is captured as a model obligation rather than remaining implicit.

changes.description: Replaced the objective-only DriveCar use case stub with an elaborated use case that performs an explicit nominal action flow, making the operational objective behaviourally defined.

Added a behavioural decomposition for the DriveCar use case using leaf actions and a composite action with explicit succession steps for start, mode selection, acceleration, monitoring, steering, and stopping.
Added a requirement that the DriveCar use case must be behaviourally elaborated, ensuring the newly introduced action flow is justified as a completeness obligation in the model.

fix.title: Replace bare Real attributes with explicit unit-bearing value types
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness issue by introducing explicit quantity/value types for all previously bare `Real` attributes and updating all affected attributes, constraints, requirements, and instance values to use those types consistently. The model now makes units and dimensionless quantities explicit for mass, speed, power, displacement, wheel dimensions, cost, voltage, current, and driver experience, eliminating ambiguity in quantitative interpretation and making constraints and requirement thresholds analyzable and consistent.

changes.description: Introduced explicit unit-bearing value types and replaced every bare `Real` attribute cited in the finding with the appropriate typed value. Updated all related constraints, requirements, and example instance assignments to dereference `.value`, ensuring units are explicit and quantitative semantics are unambiguous throughout the model.

fix.title: Bind requirement constraints to analysable vehicle attributes via verification analysis contexts
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness deficiency by introducing explicit parametric analysis structures and verification usage contexts that bind requirement variables to concrete vehicle attributes. `PerformanceReq` and `SafetyReq` remain quantitative requirements, but now their evaluation is connected to analysable attribute networks through dedicated analysis part definitions (`AccelerationAnalysis`, `BrakingSafetyAnalysis`) and verification cases (`PerformanceVerification`, `SafetyVerification`) that reference an actual `Vehicle` subject. The updated requirement constraints use analysis result attributes instead of isolated expressions, enabling formal evaluation against instantiated designs such as `myCar`.

changes.description: Updated `PerformanceReq` from a directly embedded structural expression to a requirement bound through an analysis result attribute, enabling evaluation through a parametric usage context.
Updated `SafetyReq` to depend on an analysable braking metric attribute rather than an isolated expression using wheel multiplicity, so it can be bound in a verification context.

Added explicit parametric analysis part definitions and verification usage contexts that bind vehicle attributes to computed analysis values and then bind those values into the requirements for formal evaluation.

fix.title: Add functional actions and allocate them to vehicle physical components
fix.description: This fix resolves the allocation completeness deficiency by introducing explicit functional elements as SysML v2 action definitions and adding allocate relationships from those functions to the physical parts that realize them. The model now supports traceability from intended vehicle behavior to implementing structure, specifically linking propulsion, torque transfer, and road contact functions to the engine, transmission, and wheels.

changes.description: Added explicit functional elements as action definitions so the model contains concrete functions that can be allocated to structure.
Added functional usages inside the vehicle instance and allocated them to the physical parts that realize them, creating direct function-to-structure traceability.
Updated the package header comment to reflect that the model now includes functional-to-physical allocations.
changes.before:
/**
    * Basic vehicle model demonstrating SysML v2.0 syntax
    * including parts, attributes, ports, constraints, and requirements
    */
changes.after:
/**
    * Basic vehicle model demonstrating SysML v2.0 syntax
    * including parts, attributes, ports, constraints, requirements
    * and functional-to-physical allocations
    */

fix.title: Add end-to-end operational and off-nominal mission scenarios for vehicle use
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness gap by replacing the undeveloped use case-only treatment with explicit mission/scenario behavior that exercises the full vehicle system across startup, normal driving, transition, shutdown, and degraded operation. It keeps the existing DriveCar use case, adds scenario-focused requirements to make the coverage intentional and traceable, and introduces mission actions with ordered successions for nominal and off-nominal operation so the model supports end-to-end operational validation.

changes.description: Retained the DriveCar use case and augmented the model with explicit operational scenario coverage instead of leaving the use case as an undeveloped stub.
Added atomic operational actions for startup, driving, stopping, shutdown, fault detection, and safe-state entry so the scenario model has concrete executable steps.
Added a nominal mission thread and an off-nominal degraded mission with explicit ordered successions to cover full-system operation, transitions, shutdown, and safe degraded behavior.
Added an operational-scenarios container action and a concrete usage binding the scenarios to the existing driver and vehicle instances for direct model execution/validation context.

fix.title: Add explicit verification methods and linked verification cases for requirements
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness finding by updating both requirements to include explicit verification method metadata and by linking each requirement to a dedicated verification case that defines how compliance will be established. `PerformanceReq` is assigned verification by test, and `SafetyReq` is assigned verification by analysis. The model now contains directly traceable verification elements for both requirements, enabling credible verification planning.

changes.description: Added a simple verification method type and two verification case definitions so requirements have explicit linked verification artifacts.
Updated `PerformanceReq` to include explicit verification method metadata and a `verify` link to the corresponding verification case.
Updated `SafetyReq` to include explicit verification method metadata and a `verify` link to the corresponding verification case.

fix.title: Add explicit vehicle operational modes and scenario/use-case coverage for startup, shutdown, transitions, degraded, and safe-state behavior
fix.description: This fix resolves the completeness gap by extending the model with explicit operational mode state modeling plus use cases for startup, shutdown, normal driving, degraded operation, and safe-state response. It also adds scenario coverage requirements tied to the Vehicle subject so the model now contains concrete behavioral and operational artifacts for nominal and off-nominal behavior, enabling transition analysis and future verification linkage.

changes.description: Expanded the package documentation to reflect the addition of operational mode and scenario coverage artifacts.
changes.before:
/**
* Basic vehicle model demonstrating SysML v2.0 syntax
* including parts, attributes, ports, constraints, and requirements
*/
changes.after:
/**
* Basic vehicle model demonstrating SysML v2.0 syntax
* including parts, attributes, ports, constraints, requirements,
* use cases, and operational mode/scenario coverage
*/

changes.description: Added operational state/mode modeling to Vehicle, including startup, shutdown, normal operation, degraded operation, safe state, and explicit transitions between them.
Added explicit scenario coverage requirements for startup, shutdown, and degraded/safe-state behavior so these operational cases are now represented as required model content.
Added use cases covering startup, shutdown, degraded operation, and safe-state entry to complement the state-based behavior and provide explicit operational scenario coverage.
Initialized the new operational state guard attributes on the example vehicle instance so the added behavior model is directly usable.

fix.title: Add verification cases, scenarios, methods, and evidence links for vehicle requirements
fix.description: This update resolves the absence of verification artefacts by introducing explicit verification method metadata on each requirement, concrete verification case definitions, executable-style test/analysis scenarios, and evidence-bearing model elements linked to the requirements they verify. The model now supports verification planning and requirement closure traceability.

changes.description: Added explicit verification method metadata to both requirements so each requirement identifies how it is to be verified.
Added verification scenarios, verification case definitions, and an evidence element definition to support requirement verification planning and evidence traceability.
Added concrete verification usages for the vehicle instance, including links from verification cases to the requirements they verify, execution scenarios, and evidence objects.

fix.title: Add operational behavior models for vehicle startup, driving, braking, shutdown, and mode control
fix.description: This fix resolves the behaviour completeness deficiency by introducing production-ready behavioural elements for the vehicle’s significant functions. It adds action definitions for startup, propulsion, braking, and shutdown; a composed driving action that delegates to those major functions; a vehicle state machine covering operational modes and transitions; and a sequence-style interaction for the DriveCar use case. These additions provide analyzable dynamic behaviour for startup, shutdown, propulsion, braking, and driving modes directly tied to the existing Vehicle and DriveCar context.

changes.description: Extended the Vehicle definition with operational state attributes needed to support behavioural modeling of startup, propulsion, braking, and shutdown.

Added action definitions for the major vehicle functions identified by the review: startup, propulsion, braking, shutdown, and an orchestrating driving action with typed inputs, outputs, and behavioural body.
Added a vehicle operational state machine with reachable states, explicit transitions, triggers, and effects to model driving modes and startup/shutdown logic.
Updated the DriveCar use case to include executable behaviour via a delegated driving action and an interaction sequence covering startup, drive, brake, and shutdown scenario flow.
Updated the example vehicle instance with initial operational values for the newly introduced behavioural attributes.

results.name: Completeness
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: A basic vehicle model intended to represent a road vehicle with core structural elements, simple quantitative constraints, two example requirements, and one driving use case.
- Operational domain: Automotive.
- Lifecycle phase: Early concept / preliminary design. The model contains high-level structure and constraints but limited interface, behaviour, allocation, and verification detail.
- Model scope: A partial system model / illustrative fragment rather than a complete vehicle system model. It includes selected structure, constraints, requirements, a use case, and one example instance.
- Known scope limitations: The header comment states it is a “Basic vehicle model demonstrating SysML v2.0 syntax including parts, attributes, ports, constraints, and requirements,” which indicates exemplar/tutorial intent. However, no explicit exclusions are declared in-model for behaviour, verification, allocation, or context; therefore completeness checks still apply, but findings are assessed with that limited scope in mind.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-001]
Element:        VehicleModel::PerformanceReq, VehicleModel::SafetyReq
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        The defined requirements have no `satisfy` or `verify` relationships and are not explicitly marked as pending or TBD.
Evidence:       `requirement def PerformanceReq { ... }` and `requirement def SafetyReq { ... }` are present, but the model contains no `satisfy`, `verify`, or pending/TBD annotation anywhere.
Score:          0.70  -  Requirements without satisfaction or verification links are likely to block traceability and downstream validation.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         It is not possible to trace which design elements satisfy these requirements or how they will be verified, weakening design assurance and requirement closure.
Recommendation: Add `satisfy` relationships from relevant structural/behavioural elements to each requirement and `verify` relationships to verification cases, or explicitly mark unresolved requirements as pending.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-002]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          1. Requirements Coverage
Finding:        Derived requirements and non-functional requirement coverage are incomplete or absent; only two requirements are defined and they do not cover broader performance, reliability, maintainability, interoperability, or detailed safety concerns.
Evidence:       The model defines only `PerformanceReq` and `SafetyReq`. No `derivedReq`, refinement links, or additional non-functional requirements are present.
Score:          0.55  -  Decisions may be made with only partial requirement coverage, but the effect depends on intended scope.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  The model may be intentionally illustrative, so external requirement baselines are unknown.
Impact:         Important vehicle qualities may be omitted from design and analysis, leading to incomplete trade studies and verification planning.
Recommendation: Add the relevant stakeholder and derived requirements for the intended vehicle scope, including explicit non-functional requirements and their derivation/refinement relationships.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-003]
Element:        VehicleModel::Vehicle / Model-Wide
Check:          2. System Boundary
Finding:        The model lacks an explicit system context or boundary representation showing external actors, external systems, and the environment interacting with the vehicle.
Evidence:       `part def Vehicle` exists, and `use case def DriveCar` includes `actor driver : Driver`, but there is no context diagram, no external environment elements, and no explicit boundary model.
Score:          0.64  -  Missing boundary definition can lead to omitted external interactions and incomplete interface identification.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Context analysis, interface identification, and responsibility allocation across the system boundary are incomplete.
Recommendation: Add a context-level model showing the vehicle as the system of interest, external actors/systems/environment, and the boundary-crossing interfaces.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-004]
Element:        VehicleModel::Vehicle::powerOut, VehicleModel::Engine::powerPort
Check:          2. System Boundary
Finding:        External interfaces crossing the system boundary are not comprehensively identified; the model defines internal ports but does not show all boundary-facing ports or their external counterparts.
Evidence:       `Vehicle` defines `port powerOut : PowerPort;` and `Engine` defines `port powerPort : PowerPort;` but no external connected elements or boundary interface structure are defined.
Score:          0.58  -  Interface omissions can leave significant external interactions unspecified, though some structural interface intent exists.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         External integration points cannot be fully analysed for compatibility, safety, or completeness.
Recommendation: Define all vehicle boundary interfaces as ports on the top-level system and connect them to modelled external actors/systems in a context view.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-005]
Element:        VehicleModel::Transmission, VehicleModel::Wheel, VehicleModel::Driver
Check:          3. Structural Decomposition
Finding:        Several part definitions are not instantiated in the provided composition hierarchy or usage examples.
Evidence:       `Transmission` is instantiated in `myCar`, but no explicit wheel instances are given for `wheels : Wheel[4]` and `Driver` is only used as an actor in `DriveCar`, not instantiated in a structural context. More broadly, `Subsystem` is defined but never instantiated directly.
Score:          0.33  -  Uninstantiated definitions reduce completeness of the structural picture but may be acceptable for abstract reusable types.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  Some definitions may intentionally serve as reusable types rather than requiring direct instantiation.
Impact:         Structural usage coverage is incomplete, making it harder to determine whether all defined elements participate in the intended architecture.
Recommendation: Instantiate all in-scope structural elements in an explicit composition/context where needed, or document which definitions are abstract/reusable and not intended for direct instantiation.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-006]
Element:        VehicleModel::Wheel, VehicleModel::Transmission, VehicleModel::Vehicle, VehicleModel::Engine
Check:          3. Structural Decomposition
Finding:        Leaf-level components are underspecified for analysis because critical attributes such as units, bounds, and interface detail are missing.
Evidence:       Attributes such as `mass : Real;`, `power : Real;`, `diameter : Real;`, `width : Real;`, `gearRatio : Real;`, and `maxSpeed : Real;` are present as bare `Real` values with no units or min/max/nominal values.
Score:          0.62  -  Analysis can proceed incorrectly when numerical properties lack sufficient specification.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Quantitative analyses such as performance, sizing, and consistency checks are unreliable or ambiguous.
Recommendation: Add typed value properties with units and, where critical, bounds/nominal values for all analysis-relevant attributes.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Structural Decomposition  -  All declared parts in compositions are typed (`engine : Engine`, `transmission : Transmission`, `wheels : Wheel[4]`, `part myCar : Vehicle`), so no anonymous or untyped parts were found.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-007]
Element:        VehicleModel::Vehicle::powerOut, VehicleModel::Engine::powerPort
Check:          4. Interface & Connection Completeness
Finding:        Ports do not declare direction and the port/interface definition does not define directional flow semantics.
Evidence:       `port powerOut : PowerPort;` and `port powerPort : PowerPort;` are declared, while `port def PowerPort { attribute voltage : Real; attribute current : Real; ... }` contains attributes only and no direction (`in`, `out`, `inout`) or flow specification.
Score:          0.73  -  Interface analysis is likely to be wrong or blocked when directionality is absent.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Flow compatibility, causality, and interface correctness cannot be verified.
Recommendation: Add explicit port directions and define flow items or directional features in `PowerPort`.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-008]
Element:        VehicleModel::Vehicle::powerOut, VehicleModel::Engine::powerPort
Check:          4. Interface & Connection Completeness
Finding:        The declared ports are unconnected in any usage context.
Evidence:       `Vehicle` has `powerOut` and `Engine` has `powerPort`, but `myCar : Vehicle` contains no `connect` statements and the model contains no connections anywhere.
Score:          0.78  -  Unconnected interfaces strongly indicate incomplete architecture and block interaction analysis.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Internal and external energy transfer paths cannot be analysed, and interface completeness cannot be established.
Recommendation: Add `connect` relationships in the relevant usage context and either connect or explicitly justify any intentionally unused ports.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-009]
Element:        VehicleModel::PowerPort / Model-Wide
Check:          4. Interface & Connection Completeness
Finding:        No explicit interface definitions are present for significant system boundary crossings beyond a minimal `PowerPort`, and no actual boundary crossing is modelled with compatible counterpart interfaces.
Evidence:       The only port definition is `port def PowerPort { ... }`; no additional interface definitions, external systems, or boundary-level connections are provided.
Score:          0.57  -  Missing interface definitions reduce integration completeness, though the model appears early-stage.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  The significance of omitted boundary crossings depends on intended scope and whether this is only a structural subset.
Impact:         System integration assumptions remain implicit and difficult to validate.
Recommendation: Define interface types for all significant boundary interactions and use them in explicit context-level connections.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-010]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          5. Behaviour Completeness
Finding:        The model contains no behavioural models such as actions, state machines, activities, sequences, or operational state-based logic for the vehicle’s significant functions.
Evidence:       The model includes `use case def DriveCar` but no `action def`, state machine, sequence, transition, or behavioural body elements anywhere.
Score:          0.74  -  Without behaviour, operational analysis and functional validation are significantly impaired.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Functional execution, mode logic, and dynamic behaviour cannot be analysed or validated against requirements.
Recommendation: Add behavioural models for major vehicle functions such as propulsion, braking, startup, shutdown, and driving modes.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-011]
Element:        VehicleModel::DriveCar
Check:          5. Behaviour Completeness
Finding:        The use case identifies an operational objective but is not elaborated by any behavioural scenario or action flow.
Evidence:       `use case def DriveCar { actor driver : Driver; subject vehicle : Vehicle; objective { ... } }` exists without any linked actions, scenario steps, sequence, or state-based realization.
Score:          0.60  -  Use-case intent exists, but the absence of elaboration leaves operational meaning incomplete.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Stakeholder understanding of how the vehicle achieves its mission remains incomplete and cannot support behavioural verification.
Recommendation: Elaborate the use case with scenario steps, actions, sequences, or state machines showing how the objective is achieved.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-012]
Element:        VehicleModel::Vehicle::mass, VehicleModel::Vehicle::maxSpeed, VehicleModel::Engine::power, VehicleModel::Engine::mass, VehicleModel::Engine::displacement, VehicleModel::Transmission::gearRatio, VehicleModel::Transmission::efficiency, VehicleModel::Wheel::diameter, VehicleModel::Wheel::width, VehicleModel::Subsystem::mass, VehicleModel::Subsystem::cost, VehicleModel::PowerPort::voltage, VehicleModel::PowerPort::current, VehicleModel::Driver::experience
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        Attributes are typed as bare `Real` without explicit value types or units.
Evidence:       Examples include `attribute mass : Real;`, `attribute maxSpeed : Real;`, `attribute power : Real;`, `attribute diameter : Real;`, `attribute voltage : Real;`, and similar declarations throughout the model.
Score:          0.72  -  Missing units make quantitative interpretation ambiguous and likely to produce incorrect analysis.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Constraints and requirement thresholds cannot be interpreted consistently, risking invalid engineering calculations.
Recommendation: Replace bare `Real` types with unit-bearing value types or explicit quantity kinds and units.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-013]
Element:        VehicleModel::Vehicle, VehicleModel::Engine, VehicleModel::Transmission, VehicleModel::Wheel, VehicleModel::PowerPort
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        Critical numerical attributes generally lack explicit bounds such as minimum, maximum, or nominal values.
Evidence:       The model provides constraints like `mass < 2000.0` and `maxSpeed > 60.0`, but most properties (`power`, `displacement`, `gearRatio`, `width`, `voltage`, `current`, etc.) have no min/max/nominal specification.
Score:          0.56  -  Some constraints exist, but property ranges remain incomplete for robust analysis.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Trade studies, sizing checks, and consistency validation are weakened because acceptable design envelopes are not fully defined.
Recommendation: Add explicit bounds/nominal values for critical quantitative properties, especially those used in constraints and requirements.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-014]
Element:        VehicleModel::PerformanceReq, VehicleModel::SafetyReq
Check:          6. Value & Constraint Completeness
Finding:        Quantitative requirements are present, but the requirement constraints are not bound through parametric usage contexts to actual analysable attribute networks.
Evidence:       `PerformanceReq` and `SafetyReq` each contain `require constraint { ... }`, but there are no parametric bindings or usage-context bindings connecting these to verification/analysis structures.
Score:          0.48  -  The requirements are at least expressed quantitatively, but analysis integration is incomplete.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  The exact SysML v2 modelling style intended for requirement evaluation may vary, but no explicit parametric binding is visible.
Impact:         Automated or formal evaluation of requirement satisfaction against instantiated designs is limited.
Recommendation: Introduce explicit parametric/constraint usage contexts binding requirement variables to concrete system attributes for analysis.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-015]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          7. Allocation Completeness
Finding:        No allocation relationships are present between functional elements and physical elements.
Evidence:       The model contains no `allocate` relationships anywhere, and there are no explicit functional elements beyond an undeveloped use case.
Score:          0.52  -  Allocation is incomplete, though the model contains only minimal functional modelling.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         Traceability from intended functions to implementing structure is absent, limiting architecture reasoning.
Recommendation: Add functional elements and allocate them to the physical components that realize them.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-016]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          8. Scenario & Use Case Coverage
Finding:        No mission or operational scenario is defined that exercises the full system beyond a single undeveloped use case.
Evidence:       The only scenario-related element is `use case def DriveCar`, which includes an objective but no scenario sequence or mission thread.
Score:          0.61  -  Operational coverage is incomplete and may hide missing functions or interactions.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         End-to-end operational validation and coverage of stakeholder usage are not possible.
Recommendation: Add mission/scenario models covering end-to-end vehicle operation.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-017]
Element:        Model-Wide
Check:          8. Scenario & Use Case Coverage
Finding:        Startup, shutdown, transition, and off-nominal/degraded scenarios are not modelled.
Evidence:       No scenario, sequence, or state-based model elements for startup, shutdown, mode transitions, degraded operation, or safe states appear in the model.
Score:          0.66  -  Important operational gaps are likely to affect safety and verification completeness.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         The model cannot support assessment of mode transitions, failure response, or non-nominal behaviour.
Recommendation: Add explicit nominal and off-nominal scenarios, including startup, shutdown, transition, degraded, and safe-state behaviour.

ISSUE [COMPLETENESS-018]
Element:        VehicleModel::PerformanceReq, VehicleModel::SafetyReq
Check:          9. Verification Methods
Finding:        Requirements do not define verification methods such as test, analysis, demonstration, or inspection.
Evidence:       `PerformanceReq` and `SafetyReq` include `doc`, `subject`, and `require constraint`, but no verification method metadata or linked verification elements.
Score:          0.76  -  Verification planning is likely blocked when requirements lack defined methods.
Confidence:     HIGH
Impact:         It is unclear how compliance will be established, preventing credible verification planning.
Recommendation: Add verification method information to each requirement and/or link each requirement to verification cases that specify method.
